accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text; contextual claims (FTC, Youville, COPPA, Section 230) are accurate and verifiable.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the FTC/Youville/Section 230 context is accurate real-world information, not hallucination.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as a comprehensive measure to protect minors online with FTC and state enforcement.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Notes preservation of COPPA and Section 230 and FTC enforcement, though it omits the amendment to the Protecting Children in the 21st Century Act, which is a minor sub-provision.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes key carve-outs like the prohibition on requiring government ID, knowledge-based 'minor' triggers, and preservation of state laws.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states the one-year default effective date with Subtitle B at 180 days and Subtitle C at 90 days, plus 90/60-day judicial-review limits.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the essential operative effect of all six titles accurately at a faithful high level.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized by title, internally consistent, and reads clearly.

concise.why: Compact and free of padding or repetition while covering the bill's structure.

correct_entities.why: Agencies and short titles (FTC, SCREEN Act, KOSA, SMK, SPY Kids, Safer GAMING, SAFE BOTs, D.C. Circuit) are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: Figures stated (under 17, one-third content, under 13, 10-day response, 3-hour break, 180/90-day dates, 90/60-day limits) all match the bill.
